mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate surpasses 8.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals a severe economic contraction that suppresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, which amplifies equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output that directly boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week exclusively during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during mid-cycle expansions, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns across all market conditions, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.5 percent monthly forces central banks to anticipate tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.5 percent, invalidating the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and declining unemployment rates confirms a broad-based economic recovery that elevates equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and UNRATE signal yields negative forward returns when GDP growth exceeds 3 percent while unemployment remains above 7 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that rapidly erodes short-term equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during late-cycle phases.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Short-term S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 58 percent of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, negating the supply-side drag effect.
